title: Chapter Eighty-Four: Battle for Possession (Part Three)
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The rebound force was tremendous.

Yeon-shin loaded follow-up power into his right arm meridians -- the long twelve-point pathway from shoulder to index finger taking on the firmness of Jeong family dynamic technique. Preventing dislocation.

Still he was pushed back. Furrows carved through dirt. Palm force scattered in all directions, raising yellow dust. Even vision blurred.

*As expected.*

The strike's sensation was shallow. The Seventh Apostle's energy waves had surged an instant before -- she had avoided his blow from point-blank range.

But not cleanly. Her red silk thread martial robe clung wetly to her abdomen. She had not fully deflected his palm force. Her accomplished protective energy -- the barrier that should always be active -- had been pierced for an instant.

*I have acquired a strike that works in the black rank realm.*

"You created another martial art that didn't exist?" She touched her abdomen lightly. An innocent smile gathered on her lips. "Not even a direct hit yet this much... If I took it properly, it would have been hard to survive. Amazing!"

"I asked. What are your intentions?"

"You ask the obvious. To take the Grand Teacher..."

She trailed off. Her attitude had changed. He understood why.

*I have grown. Hwa-sin is with me. She cannot subdue us without getting hurt.*

"Your blood technique," he said. "I completed the verses. It's contained in my body."

He raised Demon Roaring Blood Art. Chilling energy sprouted. Formless true qi waves burst sporadically throughout his body -- explosive demonic energy wrapping him like a living shroud. His face remained calm. Not a trace of blood art's base madness.

"It's a hundred-character verse. I'll give you five characters each time you block my sneak attack."

He proposed it flatly. Trading martial art mantras for life threats. She would maintain three hundred zhang distance. Only he could approach. If she violated this, he would take one of her arms.

"Even if I die, I can take one of your limbs."

The Seventh Apostle laughed.

"What did you say?"

But she was considering it. Twenty chances at a killing strike against a master several levels above. She could not kill him -- her mad obsession proved that. Yet capturing him would mean facing the joint suicide attack of Lightning Genius and White Qilin.

*The initiative has changed.*

"Six characters," she said.

"I'm not a merchant."

"Six."

"Five."

"Good." Her smile brightened. "I'll get to fully taste my Grand Teacher's blade?"

Wind blew between the three of them. The sun lost its heat. Trees near the road shed their greenness, leaves scattering in a dry wind of energy waves.

Yeon-shin withdrew the blood art slowly.

"Go."

"See you later."

Her pure blood robe swayed red. She moved away with casual body technique -- one moment there, the next a distant crimson figure.

"A storm blew from the start," Hwa-sin said quietly.

"She accepted because it's a reasonable distance."

"Your guts are remarkable. I thought we'd have to fight to the death right here."

"Evil faction groups are selfish. Reluctant unless it's a fight they can win more than ninety percent." Yeon-shin watched the distant red shape settle. "We must strike while she can't manifest regenerative powers."

"Naturally I must accompany you. Don't worry."

Hwa-sin's eyes were clear. They had shared life and death as companions now.

"We should be able to return alive this time too," Yeon-shin said. "The mission should succeed as well."

"Of course."

Variables from the first day. Thus began the strange journey.

---

Seven days and nights elsewhere, Flourishing Spirit Flower Master Namgung Mi had left her main family with six Flourishing Spirit Flower warriors -- won with one gesture and a smile.

She did not bring servants. She made the warriors into servants instead. The magic of a powerful and beautiful direct line young lady.

They arrived at Wuchang in Huguang Province. A post station city. Two more prefectures to Xiangyang.

They moved according to plan. The wooden signboard marked *Virtuous Teacher Sect* shattered to pieces.

The sect leader's head flew off with one sword strike while shouting. Namgung Mi's blade froze the roof ridge. Under the falling sunlight, cold energy rising from her body turned into white steam. An immortal maiden wrapped in ice. No one could face her.

Less than half an hour to achieve extermination.

In the marketplace afterward, a Flourishing Spirit Flower warrior raised the severed head high: "Here, Namgung family's Flourishing Spirit Flower Master has taken the evil sect leader's head!"

Cheers erupted. People of all ages praised the Namgung family.

Then a voice, already distant, cutting through: "These vicious wretches! How could you kill even innocent servants!"

Some young man. Running very fast, with strange dignity in his bearing.

The crowd murmured. Public sentiment shifts quickly when powerful families wield authority.

Namgung Mi's smile only widened.

*Worthless things.*

She sent a brief sound transmission. *Have it silenced.* A warrior nodded and began to move.

But in a corner of a distant inn, two people were already whispering.

"Young Hero Namgung."

"You did well. This is how one must deal with my sister. I suffered much since childhood. Waiting a day at the post station was worth it."

"I wonder if there's a reason to delay the martial matches."

"Her temperament seems to have changed troublesomely..."

"I felt uncontrolled yin energy."

"Indeed. Since energy achievement wouldn't change suddenly, she must have eaten something powerful. When deliberately raising power it seems even approaching would be difficult... Why do you say that?"

"Ten seconds."

"Hmm?"

"Perhaps ten seconds would be enough."

The boy said it with the calm certainty of a blade already drawn.

---
